explanation: No indicators of compromise. All file entropies are in normal range (3.5-5.5). No extension changes detected. Legitimate processes running (WINWORD, POWERPNT, cleanmgr, TiWorker, MsMpEng). Windows Defender active and reporting clean scans. File operations are consistent with normal office work and system maintenance.

Subtle anomalies detected consistent with semantic_shuffle pattern. Multiple document files show small size_delta changes (file_0009.xlsx +282, file_0008.xlsx +651, file_0000.xlsx -58, file_0002.pdf -73, file_0006.docx +150). No extension changes and all entropies remain below 6.5 (range 3.5-5.4), but the pattern of small modifications across multiple documents without clear user activity (no Office processes in current window) is suspicious. Content modifications without extension changes warrant continued monitoring.

Clear ransomware indicators: sleeper encryption pattern detected. Three files encrypted across three time windows - file_0023.png.locked (t-2), file_0004.xlsx.locked (t-1), file_0016.txt.locked (current). File entropy of 7.56 bits/byte on .locked file confirms encryption. Pattern of 1 file encrypted per window is consistent with sleeper ransomware that avoids detection by encrypting slowly. Extension changes (.png->.png.locked, .xlsx->.xlsx.locked, .txt->.txt.locked) are definitive ransomware signatures. Attack is at advanced stage but slow pace allows quarantine before mass encryption.

Active blitz ransomware attack in progress. Overwhelming evidence: (1) Windows Defender disabled via registry (DisableAntiSpyware) with Event ID 5001 confirming real-time protection off. (2) Mass file encryption across all user directories - current window shows 17+ files encrypted with .cry extension. Previous windows show .enc and .locked extensions, indicating escalating attack phases. (3) Encrypted files show entropy 7.52-7.91 bits/byte, confirming strong encryption. (4) File content is pure ciphertext (no valid file headers). (5) Attack is actively encrypting files in real-time across Documents, Desktop, Pictures, Downloads, AppData, and ProgramData. Immediate blocking required to halt ongoing encryption.
